name: Russian Black Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix rye flour and white flour.
2. In a large bowl, mix 400 g of the flour mixture with sugar, salt, bran cereal, caraway seeds, instant coffee, onion powder, fennel seed, and yeast.
3. Combine 560 ml water, vinegar, molasses, chocolate, and margarine in a saucepan. Heat to very warm (120-130°C) without boiling. Margarine and chocolate do not need to melt.
4. Gradually add the cereal mixture to the liquid mixture, beating for 2 minutes at medium speed while scraping the bowl occasionally.
5. Add another 75 g of the flour mixture, then beat on high for 2 minutes, continuing to scrape the bowl occasionally. Adjust with additional flour until a soft dough forms. Turn onto lightly floured surface and cover. Let rise for 15 minutes.
6. Knead the dough smooth and elastic (10-15 minutes). The dough may be sticky. Shape into a ball and place in a greased bowl. Grease the top of the dough, then cover and let rise until doubled (about one hour).
7. Punch down the dough and turn onto a lightly floured surface. Divide into two equal pieces. Shape into 12.5 cm diameter balls and place in greased round cake pans. Grease the tops of the loaves, then cover and let rise until doubled.
8. Bake at 180°C for 45-50 minutes.
9. Mix cornstarch with cold water in a small saucepan. Cook over medium heat, stirring, until it boils (about 1 minute).
10. As soon as the bread is done, glaze with the cornstarch mixture and bake an additional 2-3 minutes until set.
